h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, grass is food, rabbit is eater, so this one flips to `grass -> rabbit`.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically check and correct each arrow on the worksheet based on the food-to-eater rule.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner breakdown or resistance to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner breakdown or resistance to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner breakdown or resistance to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: the label is doing real work
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to express dissatisfaction with identical numbers while gradually accepting the labels' utility.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured. Before the worksheet gets numbers, sort the two 2s by label.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's persistent dissatisfaction by shifting from worksheet labels to physical manipulation of string and slips of paper.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no contingent adaptation to a revised framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no contingent adaptation to a revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured. Before the worksheet gets numbers, sort the two 2s by label.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's persistent dissatisfaction by shifting from worksheet labels to physical manipulation of string and slips of paper.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured. Before the worksheet gets numbers, sort the two 2s by label.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's persistent dissatisfaction by shifting from worksheet labels to physical manipulation of string and slips of paper.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: the side factor gets counted twice
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to resist the grid's representation of area scaling.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's emotional resistance ('dislike') and uses it to pivot the affective register while maintaining focus on the mathematical count.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn is identified, so there is no contingent tutor uptake of a revised framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn is identified, so there is no contingent tutor uptake of a revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's emotional resistance ('dislike') and uses it to pivot the affective register while maintaining focus on the mathematical count.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's emotional resistance ('dislike') and uses it to pivot the affective register while maintaining focus on the mathematical count.
